app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.title: Unversioned localStorage key
app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.message: localStorage.setItem("mindsquare_students", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "mindsquare_students:v1").
app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.help: Put a version in the storage key (e.g. "myKey:v1"). If you change the data shape later, old saved data can be ignored instead of crashing the app.

app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.title: Spread copy before sort()
app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.message: This wastes work because [...array].sort() copies the array just to sort it, so use array.toSorted() to sort without the extra copy (ES2023)
app.js\u0000373d33013786bea291e1dffbb2e0.help: Use `array.toSorted()` (ES2023) instead of `[...array].sort()` so you sort without copying the array first

chess-engine.js\u00007c03aa7560652f45ddd.title: Repeated inline style writes
chess-engine.js\u00007c03aa7560652f45ddd.message: This makes the browser recalculate layout again & again because you set several element.style properties in a row, so set them all at once with cssText or a CSS class to redraw once
chess-engine.js\u00007c03aa7560652f45ddd.help: Do all your reads first, then all your writes. Mixing them inside a loop makes the browser recalculate the layout again and again, which is slow

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: Array lookup inside a loop
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: These 5 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: These 6 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: Independent awaits run sequentially
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: These 4 sequential await statements run one after another even though they look independent, so the page waits longer than it needs to. Run them together with Promise.all() instead
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Use `const [a, b] = await Promise.all([fetchA(), fetchB()])` so independent calls run at the same time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: await inside a loop
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: Chained array iterations
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Combine `.map().filter()` style chains into one pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop, so you only loop over the list once

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: await inside a loop

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.title: await inside a loop
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
server.js\u00006f0290e86b303a11a54f34d8f.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

ui-handlers.js\u00002e1093a1df51805ceccd.title: Non-passive scroll listener
ui-handlers.js\u00002e1093a1df51805ceccd.message: "scroll" listener without { passive: true } makes scrolling janky for your users. Only add it if the handler doesn't call event.preventDefault(), since passive listeners silently ignore preventDefault().
ui-handlers.js\u00002e1093a1df51805ceccd.help: Add `{ passive: true }` as the third argument: `addEventListener('scroll', handler, { passive: true })`. Only do this if the handler doesn't call `event.preventDefault()`, since passive listeners ignore it (which breaks pull-to-refresh, custom gestures, and nested scrolling).

server.js\u0000cd457b2686b014426bd4fef90.title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
server.js\u0000cd457b2686b014426bd4fef90.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
server.js\u0000cd457b2686b014426bd4fef90.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between
